title: salix rigida var. vestita
intro: Salix rigida var. vestita, also known as the stiffer willow, is a shrubby willow plant native to Russia, China, and Japan. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its rich content of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Salix rigida var. vestita is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, and has been used in herbal medicine for centuries to treat various ailments.
